commit.message:
Ecology system: reaction-diffusion, temperature, chemotaxis, depth, mood, palettes, presets

Major feature expansion — 7 new systems, all independently controllable:

1. Reaction-Diffusion (Gray-Scott): GPU compute with 5-point Laplacian,
   dye coupling + velocity force from RD gradients. 4 iterations/frame.
   Params: rdAmount, rdFeedRate, rdKillRate, rdDyeAmount, rdForceAmount, rdScale

2. Temperature/Buoyancy: Semi-Lagrangian temp advection, buoyancy force
   creates convection cells. Heat injected with dye splats.
   Params: tempAmount, tempBuoyancy, tempDissipation, tempDyeTint

3. Chemotaxis: 32x32 dye proxy grid, flockers follow dye gradients.
   Param: flockChemotaxis

4. Depth/Parallax: Display shader samples dye at parallax-offset UVs.
   Params: depthAmount, depthSpeed

5. Mood Lighting: Slow warm/cool color temperature cycling.
   Params: moodAmount, moodSpeed

6. Color Palettes: 50 embedded palettes (5 scientific colormaps +
   45 curated community palettes). Maps to base/accent/tip/glitter/sheen.
   Param: paletteIndex

7. Preset Browser: Left/right arrows cycle through saved examples.

All systems zero-cost when disabled (gated by > 0.01 checks).
D=81 (D_MOTION=60, D_COLOR=21). 16 new BO parameters.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: collections index template, chart empty-state and Y-axis

Bug 1: /collections/ was rendering GP's default template. Added
templates/archive-collections.php (full collection card grid with name,
description snippet, and count) and wired is_page('collections') into
template_include, body_class, and enqueue — matching the existing
/names/ index pattern.

Bug 2: Chart Y-axis showed decimals (#0.1–#1) when all seed names
have rank 0. PHP now checks for at least one non-zero rank value in
the history JSON before rendering the chart block. When all values
are 0 the chart is replaced with a styled rare-name notice instead.
JS also guards against all-null data points (belt and suspenders) and
hides the chart wrap if reached. Y-axis now uses precision:0 (whole
numbers only), reverse:true (rank #1 at top), and computed min/max
bounds with padding so the line never hugs the axis edges.

commit.message:
fix(fae): two-pass speaker matching — fae_self no longer shadows owner (v0.8.89)

Root cause: SpeakerProfileStore.match() returned the highest similarity
across ALL profiles including fae_self. Since voice cloning produces
embeddings nearly identical to the owner's voice, fae_self always won
at ~0.999 similarity, preventing the owner's real profile from matching.

Fix: Two-pass matching in PipelineCoordinator:
1. Match against human profiles only (excludingRoles: [.faeSelf])
2. If no human match, check fae_self separately for echo detection

Also: fae_self echo rejection now uses EchoSuppressor.isInSuppression
(duration-proportional) instead of a fixed 10s window from speech start.
This prevents blocking the user from speaking 5+ seconds after Fae
finishes a short response.

New SpeakerProfileStore methods:
- match(excludingRoles:) — filter profiles by role during matching
- matchesFaeSelf() — dedicated fae_self echo detection check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: binary operator comment preservation (+2 tests)

Add operatorLeadingComments, operatorTrailingComments, and
operatorHasPrecedingLineBreak to BinaryExpression AST node. Capture
these in parseBinaryExpression and parseBinaryExpressionRest. In
emitBinaryExpression, when the operator has a preceding line break:
emit comments on their own indented lines before the operator, then
the operator at the start of the next line, then any inline comments
after the operator, then the right operand (on a new line if source
shows a newline after the last comment/operator).

Fixes commentOnBinaryOperator1 and commentOnBinaryOperator2 tests.

Fix: SW serving offline HTML for Railway API calls — crash on .map()

Service worker was intercepting cross-origin (Railway) requests and
replacing CORS failures with /offline.html, causing res.data to be
HTML instead of JSON and crashing with 'd.map is not a function'.
Added early return for non-same-origin requests. Also added Array.isArray
guards in searchUsers, getProofreaders, getAllVolunteerRequests.

Switch prod to file sessions and sync queue for reliability

In-container Redis causes intermittent 500 errors when it's not ready
or drops connections. File-based sessions and sync queue are more
reliable for single-container Railway deployments. Can be overridden
via env vars if an external Redis is preferred.

#588 Fix: use pending name cache instead of pre-creating user in /api/init

Previous approach caused StaleDataError (duplicate users) because _ensure_session_exists
created a user with tenant-prefixed session_id while services.py created another without prefix.

New approach:
- /api/init stores user_name in _pending_user_names cache (session_id -> name)
- services.py get_or_create_session() pops pending name and passes it to get_or_create_user()
- _ensure_session_exists() also checks pending names as fallback
- No duplicate users, no StaleDataError

commit.message:
Fix Fantasy GW: use league-wide gameweek, no club dependency

The active gameweek query was tied to clubId from ClubProvider. Users
without a followed club got no clubId → query disabled → fallback to
GW1. Even users WITH clubs saw a flash of GW1 while ClubProvider loaded.

Fix: new getLeagueActiveGameweek() queries MIN(active_gameweek) across
all clubs — no club membership required. useLeagueActiveGameweek() fires
immediately on mount, same GW for every user. Removed all clubId/
clubLoading dependencies from the gameweek initialization path.

Fix: invalidate backend cache when admin saves trail

Admin panel writes directly to Supabase, bypassing the backend API, so
the backend's TTL cache (10 min) never knew about changes — mobile kept
showing stale cover images. Added DELETE /trails/:id/cache endpoint
(protected by service key) and call it from saveTrail after each update
so changes appear immediately in the app.

(comment on every post). The setting is adjustable via the template
editor UI and persisted in groups_meta.json.

commit.message:
fix: use Updates map instead of chained Update calls in member repository

Chained .Update() calls executed separate SQL queries, and result
only reflected the last one. If the last field was unchanged,
RowsAffected=0 caused "member not found" error, preventing
CheckProfileComplete from running.

  scroll gallery with fullscreen viewer on detail

fix: retry with backoff for task title generation (#266)

* task: move inconsistent-task-title-generation to active

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add retry with exponential backoff to task title generation

Under burst load (multiple concurrent tasks), Workers AI may rate-limit
or respond slowly, causing title generation to fail silently and fall
back to message truncation. This adds:

- Retry loop with configurable max retries (default 2) and exponential
  backoff (default 1s base delay)
- Error classification (timeout, rate_limit, error) for better logging
- Per-attempt logging with attempt number and error category
- New env vars: TASK_TITLE_MAX_RETRIES, TASK_TITLE_RETRY_DELAY_MS
- Tests for retry success, retry exhaustion, and error classification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* task: archive inconsistent-task-title-generation (resolved)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review feedback for task title retry

- Skip retries on timeout (only retry rate_limit and generic errors) —
  retrying when Workers AI is slow wastes more of the 30s wall-clock budget
- Move Agent/model construction outside retry loop (no per-attempt state)
- Add retryMaxDelayMs cap (default 4s) to prevent unbounded backoff
- Add tests: timeout not retried, rate limit retried, empty response not
  retried, mixed failure sequence, delay cap behavior
- New env var: TASK_TITLE_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_MS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Strengthen bash commandments: ban cd entirely, ban git -C

The previous rules only banned `cd` to the current directory, which
Claude interpreted as not applying when working in worktrees. Now:
- `cd` is banned in all Bash commands (use Bash tool's working directory)
- `&&` and `;` chaining banned unconditionally
- `git -C` banned (breaks permission pattern matching)
- Worktree example added to close the loophole

Streamlit dashboard analyzing trading volume dependencies across
MOEX instrument classes (stocks, bonds, futures, options, currency, repo)
on market factors: volatility (RVI), trend (ADX), IMOEX, market cap,
CPI, USD/RUB, CNY/RUB, M2 money supply.

- Weekly aggregation with 9 analytical factors

- Supabase PostgreSQL for intermediate storage
- Docker + Streamlit Community Cloud deployment

commit.message:
feat: seed default owner admin in migration 0037

Adds a production-safe idempotent migration that creates a default
admin account (admin@example.com / AdminPass1) so the app is
accessible after first deploy without manual DB setup.

Hash is bcrypt+sha512 with 12 iterations, compatible with
buddy.hashers/check used in admin auth. ON CONFLICT (email)
DO NOTHING makes the migration safe to re-run.

IMPORTANT: Change the password after first login.

commit.message:
Move KubeVirt infra pods to dedicated nodes, free ~685Mi on cp-1

KubeVirt infra components (virt-operator, virt-api, virt-controller)
were consuming ~685Mi on cp-1 despite no VMs running. Added
infra.nodePlacement with kubevirt=true nodeSelector so they only
schedule on dedicated kubevirt worker nodes.

fix: add callback bridge HTML for implicit-grant flows, fix workspace dead code

Auth: Add _render_auth_callback_bridge_html() that serves a client-side
bridge page when /auth/callback receives no server-visible params. This
handles Supabase implicit-grant and magic-link flows where the access
token arrives in the URL hash fragment (invisible to the server). The
bridge extracts it and POSTs to /auth/token-exchange.

Workspace: Move _require_membership_or_error from dead code position
(after return router) to before first use. Relax POST /workspaces to
auto-generate a timestamped name when no name is provided, allowing
frontends to create workspaces without a body.
